claim: For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, n^3 - n is divisible by 2.
For all integers n, the product (n-1)n(n+1) is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n^3 is congruent to n modulo 3.
For all integers n, n^3 is congruent to n modulo 2.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 30.
For all integers n, n^5 is congruent to n modulo 5.
For all integers n, n^5 - n is divisible by 6.
For all integers n, n(n-1)(n+1)(n^2+1) is divisible by 30.
For all integers n, n^7 - n is divisible by 42.
For all integers n, n^7 is congruent to n modulo 7.
For all integers n, n^9 - n is divisible by 30.
For all integers n, n^11 - n is divisible by 66.
For all integers n, gcd(n,n^3-n) equals abs(n).
For all integers n, gcd(n-1,n^3-n) is at least abs(n-1).
For all integers n, gcd(n+1,n^3-n) is at least abs(n+1).
For all odd integers n, n^2-1 is divisible by 8.
For all odd integers n, n^3-n is divisible by 24.
For all odd integers n, vp(n^2-1,2) is at least 3.
For all even integers n, n^3-n is divisible by 24.
For all even integers n, vp(n^3-n,2) is at least 3.
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by phi(7).
For all integers n, tau(abs(n^3-n)+1) is at least 2.
For all integers n, sigma(abs(n^3-n)+1) is greater than abs(n^3-n)+1.
For all prime integers n, n^4-1 is divisible by 5.
For all prime integers n greater than 5, n^4 is congruent to 1 modulo 5.
For all prime integers n greater than 7, n^6 is congruent to 1 modulo 7.
For all prime integers n greater than 3, n^2-1 is divisible by 24.
For all prime integers n greater than 3, n^3-n is divisible by 24.
For all prime integers n greater than 3, vp(n^2-1,2) is at least 3.
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by gcd(n-1,n+1).
For all integers n, one of n-1, n, n+1 is divisible by 3.
For all integers n, at least one of n-1 and n+1 is even.
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by tau(6).
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by phi(9).
For all integers n, n^5-n is divisible by phi(31).
For all integers n, n^7-n is divisible by phi(43).
For all integers n, n^11 is congruent to n modulo 11.
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by the product of the distinct prime divisors of 6.
For all odd integers n, n^4 is congruent to 1 modulo 16.
For all odd integers n, n^8 is congruent to 1 modulo 16.
For all odd integers n, n^2 is congruent to 1 modulo 8.
For all integers n, n^10-n^2 is divisible by 30.
For all integers n, n^14-n^2 is divisible by 42.
For all integers n, n(n^2-1)/6 is an integer.
For all integers n, tau(abs(n*(n^2-1)/6)+1) is at least 1.
For all integers n, n^3-n factors as n(n-1)(n+1).
For all integers n, every divisor of n divides n^3-n.
For all integers n, every divisor of n-1 divides n^3-n.
For all integers n, every divisor of n+1 divides n^3-n.
For all integers n, n^3-n is divisible by sigma(3).
For all integers n, n^9 is congruent to n modulo 9.
For all integers n, n^11-n is divisible by 22.
For all integers n, n^5-n is divisible by 10.
For all integers n, n^7-n is divisible by 14.
